title: Draft: AI Skills Age Fast
description: Draft carousel generated automatically from a YouTube transcript about frontier operations, calibration, and how AI skills expire faster than traditional workforce skills.

slides.title: Most AI skills now expire on a quarterly cycle.
body: That’s the real shift.
This isn’t like learning Excel, coding, or some fixed tool once and being done.
The target keeps moving.

slides.title: The useful metaphor is an expanding bubble.
body: Inside the bubble: everything agents can do reliably today.
Outside the bubble: everything that still needs a person.
The surface is where the interesting work happens.

slides.title: The skill is not learning the boundary once.
body: The skill is maintaining calibration as the boundary keeps shifting.
If your mental model is six months old, you’re probably underusing AI in some places and overtrusting it in others.
Both are expensive.

slides.title: The best operators don’t just know that agents fail.
body: They know the specific texture of failure at the current capability level.
That matters more than generic skepticism.
You need differentiated checks for different kinds of work.

slides.title: Old failure patterns go stale fast.
body: One model generation struggled to retrieve details from deep inside long documents.
A few months later, retrieval performance jumped dramatically.
If you didn’t update your boundary sense, your workflow is already wrong.

slides.title: This is why the real bottleneck becomes human attention.
body: As agents get better, the problem shifts from doing the work to deciding what deserves a human review.
You cannot review everything at the same depth.
So attention allocation becomes part of the skill.

slides.title: The winning skill is frontier operations.
body: Boundary sensing.
Seam design.
Failure model maintenance.
Capability forecasting.
Leverage calibration.
